questions.question_text: Glycerol is
questions.question_html: Glycerol is
questions.explanation: Glycerol and trihydroxypropane are common names. The IUPAC name is propane-1,2,3-triol. The common name glycerol comes from the root glyco- which means sweet. Glucose, glycogen, and glycerin (another name for glycerol) have the same etymology.

questions.question_text: Isopropyl alcohol on oxidation forms:
questions.question_html: Isopropyl alcohol on oxidation forms:
questions.explanation: Secondary alcohols are oxidized to ketones. The oxidation of isopropyl alcohol by potassium dichromate (K 2 Cr 2 O 7 ) gives acetone, the simplest ketone. Unlike aldehydes, ketones are relatively resistant to further oxidation, so no special precautions are required to isolate them as they form.

questions.question_text: Give IUPAC names of the following compound:
questions.question_html: Give IUPAC names of the following compound:
questions.explanation: Lowest set of locants: The lowest set of locants is defined as the set that, when compared term by term with other locant sets, each cited in order of increasing value, has the lowest term at the first point of difference. With regard to numbering of locants, simple prefixes (simple substituent groups consisting of just one part that describes an atom, or group of atoms as a unit, for example methyl and ethoxy) are considered together with equal seniority: Therefore, the given compound is named as 2-ethoxy-1,1-dimethylcyclohexane rather than 1-ethoxy-2,2-dimethylcyclohexane since the locant set ‘1, 1, 2’ is lower than ‘1, 2, 2’.

questions.question_text: Give IUPAC names of the following compound: {tex}H - \mathop {\mathop C\limits_|^| }\limits_H^H - \mathop {\mathop C\limits_|^| }\limits_{OH}^{C{H_3}} - \mathop {\mathop C\limits_|^| }\limits_H^H - H{/tex}
questions.question_html:
Give IUPAC names of the following compound:

{tex}H - \mathop {\mathop C\limits_|^| }\limits_H^H - \mathop {\mathop C\limits_|^| }\limits_{OH}^{C{H_3}} - \mathop {\mathop C\limits_|^| }\limits_H^H - H{/tex}
questions.explanation: Longest chain has three carbons and at 2 nd position both –OH and –CH 3 group are attachted. {tex}C{H_3} - \mathop {\mathop {{}^2C}\limits_|^| }\limits_{{}^3C{H_3}}^{{}^1C{H_3}} - OH{/tex} Common name : tert.Butyl alcohol IUPAC name : 2-Methylpropan-2-ol

questions.question_text: Anisole is called
questions.question_html: Anisole is called
questions.explanation: Anisole, or methoxybenzene, is an organic compound with the formula CH 3 OC 6 H 5 . It is a colorless liquid with a smell reminiscent of anise seed, and in fact many of its derivatives are found in natural and artificial fragrances.

questions.question_text: C 6 H 5 OCH 2 CH 3 is called:
questions.question_html: C6H5OCH2CH3 is called:
questions.explanation: C 6 H 5 OCH 2 CH 3 is ethyl phenyl ether or phenetole is an organic compound that is ether. Ethyl phenyl ether has the same properties as some other ethers, such as volatility, explosive vapors, and the ability to form peroxides. IUPAC name : Ethoxybenzene Other names : Phenetole, Ethyl Phenyl Ether

questions.question_text: 3 - methyl phenol is called
questions.question_html: 3 - methyl phenol is called
questions.explanation: Meta-Cresol (m-cresol) also 3-methylphenol, is an organic compound with the formula CH 3 C 6 H 4 . It is a colourless, viscous liquid that is an intermediate in the production of other chemicals.

questions.question_text: One of the following is not a dihydroxy derivative of benzene.
questions.question_html: One of the following is not a dihydroxy derivative of benzene.
questions.explanation: Catechol is o- hydroxyl phenol or Catechol also known as pyrocatechol or 1,2- dihydroxybenzene, Resorcinol is m- hydroxyphenol Quinol is p-hydroxyphenol or benzene-1,4-diol is an aromatic organic compound that is a type of phenol, a derivative of benzene.

questions.question_text: Aldehydes react with Grignard reagent to produce______.
questions.question_html: Aldehydes react with Grignard reagent to produce______.
questions.explanation: The Grignard Reaction is the addition of an organomagnesium halide (Grignard reagent) to a ketone or aldehyde, to form tertiary or secondary alcohol, respectively. The reaction with formaldehyde leads to primary alcohol. Grignard Reagents are also used in the following important reactions: The addition of an excess of a Grignard reagent to an ester or lactone gives tertiary alcohol in which two alkyl groups are the same, and the addition of a Grignard reagent to a nitrile produces an unsymmetrical ketone via a metalloimine intermediate. RCHO + R 1 MgX {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} RCH(OH)R 1

questions.question_text: Ketones react with Grignard reagent to produce _______.
questions.question_html: Ketones react with Grignard reagent to produce _______.
questions.explanation: The Grignard Reaction is the addition of an organomagnesium halide (Grignard reagent) to a ketone or aldehyde, to form tertiary or secondary alcohol, respectively. The reaction with formaldehyde leads to primary alcohol. Grignard Reagents are also used in the following important reactions: The addition of an excess of a Grignard reagent to an ester or lactone gives tertiary alcohol in which two alkyl groups are the same, and the addition of a Grignard reagent to a nitrile produces an unsymmetrical ketone via a metalloimine intermediate. RCOR 1 + R 2 MgX {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} RC(OH)R 1 R 2

questions.question_text: Primary alcohols are prepared by the reduction of carboxylic acids. Though lithium aluminium hydride is a strong reducing agent, it is not used in the reaction. This is so because:
questions.question_html: Primary alcohols are prepared by the reduction of carboxylic acids. Though lithium aluminium hydride is a strong reducing agent, it is not used in the reaction. This is so because:

options.text: only used for secondary and tertiary alcohols
options.html: only used for secondary and tertiary alcohols

questions.explanation: Carboxylic acids are reduced to primary alcohols in excellent yields by lithium aluminium hydride, a strong reducing agent. However, LiAlH 4 is an expensive reagent, and therefore, used for preparing special chemicals only. Commercially, acids are reduced to alcohols by converting them to the esters followed by their reduction using hydrogen in the presence of a catalyst (catalytic hydrogenation).

questions.question_text: Aldehydes are reduced to the corresponding alcohols by the addition of hydrogen in the presence of catalysts to form ________.
questions.question_html: Aldehydes are reduced to the corresponding alcohols by the addition of hydrogen in the presence of catalysts to form ________.
questions.explanation: RCHO + H 2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} RCH 2 OH Aldehydes and ketones are most readily reduced with hydride reagents. The reducing agents LiAlH 4 and NaBH 4 act as a source of 4 x H - (hydride ion) Overall 2 H atoms are added across the C=O to give H-C-O-H Hydride reacts with the carbonyl group, C=O, in aldehydes or ketones to give alcohols. The substituents on the carbonyl dictate the nature of the product alcohol. Reduction of methanal (formaldehyde) gives methanol. Reduction of other aldehydes gives primary alcohol. Reduction of ketones gives secondary alcohol.

questions.question_text: Monochlorination of toluene in sunlight followed by hydrolysis by aq. NaOH yields
questions.question_html: Monochlorination of toluene in sunlight followed by hydrolysis by aq. NaOH yields
questions.explanation: Monochlorination of toluene gives benzylchloride. C 6 H 5 CH 3 + Cl 2 + hv {tex} \to {/tex} C 6 H 5 CH 2 Cl benzyl chloride on reaction with aq. NaOH will give benzyl alcohol by substitution reaction. C 6 H 5 CH 2 Cl + NaOH(aq) {tex} \to {/tex} C 6 H 5 CH 2 OH

questions.question_text: Alcohols have high boiling points because of:
questions.question_html: Alcohols have high boiling points because of:

options.text: increase in the number of carbon atoms.
options.html: increase in the number of carbon atoms.

options.text: presence of intermolecular hydrogen bonding in them.
options.html: presence of intermolecular hydrogen bonding in them.

options.text: increase in van der Waals forces.
options.html: increase in van der Waals forces.

questions.correct_option_text: presence of intermolecular hydrogen bonding in them.
questions.explanation: In alkanes, the only intermolecular forces are van der Waals dispersion forces. Hydrogen bonds are much stronger than these and therefore it takes more energy to separate alcohol molecules than it does to separate alkane molecules. That's the main reason that the boiling points are higher.

questions.question_text: Benzaldehyde differs from acetaldehyde in that:
questions.question_html: Benzaldehyde differs from acetaldehyde in that:

options.text: Benzaldehyde does not react with NaHSO 3 .
options.html: Benzaldehyde does not react with NaHSO<sub>3</sub>.

options.text: Benzaldehyde does not react with Tollens reagent.
options.html: Benzaldehyde does not react with Tollens reagent.

options.text: Benzaldehyde does not react with HCN.
options.html: Benzaldehyde does not react with HCN.

options.text: Benzaldehyde does not react with Fehling's solution.
options.html: Benzaldehyde does not react with Fehling's solution.

questions.correct_option_text: Benzaldehyde does not react with Fehling's solution.
questions.explanation: Fehling's Solution Test: When acetaldehyde is heated with a Fehling solution, the red ppt. of cuprous oxide is formed. But benzaldehyde does not give red ppt. with Fehling solution. Reaction is as follows: CH 3 CHO + 2Cu 2+ + 5OH - {tex}\to {/tex} CH 3 COO - + Cu 2 O + 3H 2 O C 6 H 5 CHO + 2Cu 2+ + 5OH - {tex}\to {/tex} No red ppt.

questions.question_text: Which of the following reactions will not result in the formation of carbon-carbon bond?
questions.question_html: Which of the following reactions will not result in the formation of carbon-carbon bond?
questions.explanation: The Cannizzaro reaction, named after its discoverer Stanislao Cannizzaro, is a chemical reaction that involves the base-induced disproportionation of an aldehyde lacking a hydrogen atom in the alpha position. This redox disproportionation of non-enolizable aldehydes to carboxylic acids and alcohols is conducted in concentrated base.

questions.question_text: IUPAC name of the following compound is {tex}C{H_3} - \mathop {\mathop C\limits_| H}\limits_{C{H_3}} - OC{H_3}{/tex} is ________.
questions.question_html: IUPAC name of the following compound is {tex}C{H_3} - \mathop {\mathop C\limits_| H}\limits_{C{H_3}} - OC{H_3}{/tex} is ________.
questions.explanation: If the oxygen is not attached to the end of the main alkane chain, then the whole shorter alkyl-plus-ether group is treated as a side-chain and prefixed with its bonding position on the main chain. Thus CH 3 OCH(CH 3 ) 2 is 2-methoxypropane.

questions.question_text: Boiling points of alcohols and phenols are higher than:
questions.question_html: Boiling points of alcohols and phenols are higher than:

options.text: Haloalkanes and haloarenes of comparable molecular masses
options.html: Haloalkanes and haloarenes of comparable molecular masses

questions.explanation: The oxygen atom of alcohol is sp 3 hybridized and has two non-bonding pairs of electrons. The O-H bond of alcohol is strongly polarized and hydrogen bonding occurs in much the same way as in water molecules. As a consequence, alcohols have relatively high boiling points compared to other organic compounds of similar molecular weight.

questions.question_text: Lucas reagent is:
questions.question_html: Lucas reagent is:
questions.explanation: Lucas reagent is a solution of anhydrous zinc chloride (Lewis acid) in concentrated hydrochloric acid. It is used as a reagent to test alcohol and classify them in accordance to their reactivity. The reaction is a substitution reaction where the chloride of the zinc chloride gets replaced by the hydroxyl group of the alcohol.

questions.question_text: Alcohols undergo dehydration (removal of a molecule of water) to form alkenes on treating with
questions.question_html: Alcohols undergo dehydration (removal of a molecule of water) to form alkenes on treating with
questions.explanation: The dehydration reaction of alcohols to generate alkene proceeds by heating the alcohols in the presence of a strong acid, such as sulfuric or phosphoric acid, at high temperatures. The required range of reaction temperature decreases with increasing substitution of the hydroxy-containing carbon: 1° alcohols: 170° - 180°C 2° alcohols: 100°– 140 °C 3° alcohols: 25°– 80°C If the reaction is not sufficiently heated, the alcohols do not dehydrate to form alkenes, but react with one another to form ethers.

questions.question_text: Alcohols are easily dehydrated in the following order:
questions.question_html: Alcohols are easily dehydrated in the following order:
questions.explanation: The rate of dehydration is related to the ease of formation of Carbocation and the energy of the carbocation intermediate. Dehydration involves the formation of carbocation 1 st and since tertiary carbocation is more stable than secondary and primary therefore 3° is dehydrated first. The ease of formation of Carbocation is Tertiary>Secondary>Primary.

questions.question_text: One of the following alcohols do not undergo oxidation reaction:
questions.question_html: One of the following alcohols do not undergo oxidation reaction:
questions.explanation: The outcome of oxidation reactions of alcohol depends on the substituents on the carbinol carbon. In order for each oxidation step to occur, there must be H on the carbinol carbon. Primary alcohols can be oxidized to aldehydes or further to carboxylic acids. In aqueous media, the carboxylic acid is usually the major product. PCC or PDC, which are used in dichloromethane, allow the oxidation to be stopped at the intermediate aldehyde. Secondary alcohols can be oxidized to ketones but no further: Tertiary alcohols cannot be oxidized (no carbinol C-H).

questions.question_text: In the reaction
questions.question_html: In the reaction

options.text: They can donate a proton to a stronger base
options.html: They can donate a proton to a stronger base

questions.explanation: Phenol reacts with sodium hydroxide solution to give a colourless solution containing sodium phenoxide. In this reaction, the hydrogen ion has been removed by the strongly basic hydroxide ion in the sodium hydroxide solution.

questions.question_text: What will be the correct order of acidity of the following compounds?
questions.question_html: What will be the correct order of acidity of the following compounds?
questions.explanation: The acidity of phenols depends on the group attached to the benzene ring. Groups showing electron withdrawing nature i.e. -I and -R effect will increase the acidity while group showing electron donating nature like +I and +R effect will decrease acidity. Resonance effect of group (-R or +R) attached to benzene system is operative only ortho and para position of the benzene system, while at meta position only inductive effect is operative. Clearly, b will be most acidic because -NO 2 group attached will show strong -R effect. In d, -NO 2 is present at meta position where only -I is effective. -I effect of -NO 2 is more than -OCH 3 group so, d will be more acidic than c, e will be least acidic as -OCH 3 group is attached at para position and shows +R effect.

questions.question_text: The reaction C 2 H 5 ONa + C 2 H 5 I {tex}\to{/tex} C 2 H 5 OC 2 H 5 + NaI is known as:
questions.question_html: The reaction C2H5ONa + C2H5I {tex}\to{/tex} C2H5OC2H5 + NaI is known as:
questions.explanation: Williamson’s synthesis : When an alkyl halide reacts with sodium alkoxide, ether is formed. This reaction is known as Williamson’s synthesis. The reaction generally follows the S N 2 mechanism for primary alcohols. {tex}\mathop {R - X + R' - \underset{\raise0.3em\hbox{$\smash{\scriptscriptstyle\cdot\cdot}$}}{\ddot O} \mathop {Na}\limits^ + \to R - \underset{\raise0.3em\hbox{$\smash{\scriptscriptstyle\cdot\cdot}$}}{\ddot O} - R' + Na\;X}\limits_{Williamson's\;synthesis} {/tex}

questions.question_text: Lucas test is associated with ________.
questions.question_html: Lucas test is associated with ________.
questions.explanation: Lucas Test is a test that is used to distinguish between primary, secondary, and tertiary alcohols. This test is carried out with the help of Lucas reagent, which is a solution of anhydrous Zinc Chloride and concentrated hydrochloric acid (ZnCl 2 + HCl). It is based on the difference between the reactivity of primary, secondary, and tertiary alcohols with hydrogen halides.

questions.question_text: An organic compound containing oxygen, upon oxidation forms a carboxylic acid as the only organic product with its molecular mass higher by 14 units. The organic compound is ________.
questions.question_html: An organic compound containing oxygen, upon oxidation forms a carboxylic acid as the only organic product with its molecular mass higher by 14 units. The organic compound is ________.
questions.explanation: When –CH 2 OH group is replaced by –COOH group then only molecular weight will increase by 14units.

questions.question_text: Arrange the following compounds in increasing order of their acid strength: Propan – 1 – ol, 2,4,6 – trinitrophenol, 3 – nitrophenol, 3,5 – dinitrophenol,phenol, 4 – methylphenol.
questions.question_html:
Arrange the following compounds in increasing order of their acid strength:
Propan – 1 – ol, 2,4,6 – trinitrophenol, 3 – nitrophenol, 3,5 – dinitrophenol,phenol, 4 – methylphenol.

options.text: 3 – nitrophenol, 3,5 – dinitrophenol,2,4, 6 – trinitrophenol, Propan – 1 – ol, 4 – methylphenol

options.text: 4 – methylphenol, phenol, propan – 1 – ol,3 – nitrophenol, 3,5 – dinitrophenol, 2,4, 6 – trinitrophenol.
options.html: 4 – methylphenol, phenol, propan – 1 – ol,3 – nitrophenol, 3,5 – dinitrophenol, 2,4, 6 – trinitrophenol.

questions.correct_option_text: Propan – 1 – ol, 4 – methylphenol, phenol, 3 – nitrophenol, 3,5 – dinitrophenol,2,4, 6 – trinitrophenol.
questions.explanation: Phenols are more acidic than alcohols Also in phenols if –M groups like –NO 2 are arranged then acidity increases. And –M operates only at o/p position. At m only –I operate.

questions.question_text: Alcoholic compounds react:
questions.question_html: Alcoholic compounds react:
questions.explanation: Alcohols as nucleophile: The bond between O−H is broken when alcohol react as nucleophiles. Alcohols as electrophile : The bond between C−O is broken when alcohol reacts as electrophiles

questions.question_text: An organic compound X is oxidized by using acidified K 2 Cr 2 O 7 . The product obtained reacts with Phenyl hydrazine but does not answer the silver mirror test. The possible structure of X is:
questions.question_html: An organic compound X is oxidized by using acidified K2Cr2O7. The product obtained reacts with Phenyl hydrazine but does not answer the silver mirror test. The possible structure of X is:
questions.explanation: Secondary alcohol on oxidation forms ketone which reacts with hydrazine bus doesn't gives a silver mirror test.

questions.question_text: One mole of an organic compound 'A' with the formula C 3 H 8 O reacts completely with two moles of HI to form X and Y. When 'Y' is boiled with aqueous alkali forms Z. Z answers the iodoform test. The compound 'A' is ______.
questions.question_html: One mole of an organic compound 'A' with the formula C3H8O reacts completely with two moles of HI to form X and Y. When 'Y' is boiled with aqueous alkali forms Z. Z answers the iodoform test. The compound 'A' is ______.
questions.explanation: Ether reacts with HI to form alcohol and alkyl iodide. Alcohol on oxidation will give the iodoform test.

questions.question_text: With dilute nitric acid at low temperature (298 K), phenol yields
questions.question_html: With dilute nitric acid at low temperature (298 K), phenol yields

options.text: a mixture of ortho and para nitro phenols
options.html: a mixture of ortho and para nitro phenols

questions.correct_option_text: a mixture of ortho and para nitro phenols
questions.explanation: Nitration of phenols: Phenols upon treatment with dilute nitric acid undergoes nitration at low temperature (298 K) to give a mixture of ortho and para nitrophenols. The mixture formed is further separated into ortho and para nitrophenols by steam distillation on the basis of their volatility. Due to intramolecular and intermolecular hydrogen bonding, ortho nitrophenols are lesser volatile in comparison to para nitrophenols which involves only intermolecular hydrogen bonding. ​​​​​​​

questions.question_text: In alcohols, an increase of branching in carbon chain results in boiling point______.
questions.question_html: In alcohols, an increase of branching in carbon chain results in boiling point______.
questions.explanation: As the length of the carbon chain increases, the surface area of the compound will also increase. Van der Waals dispersion force is proportional to the surface area. So the increase of surface increases the ability of individual molecules to attract each other. Branching in molecules decreases the surface area thereby decreasing the attractive force between individual molecules. As a result, the boiling point decreases.

questions.question_text: The compound formed as a result of oxidation of ethyl benzene by KMnO 4 is
questions.question_html: The compound formed as a result of oxidation of ethyl benzene by KMnO4 is
questions.explanation: Oxidation of aromatic alkanes with KMnO4 to give carboxylic acids. Description: Treatment of an alkylbenzene with potassium permanganate results in oxidation to give the benzoic acid. Key bonds formed Key bonds broken C-O( {tex}\pi{/tex} ) C-H C-O C-H C-OH C-H

questions.question_text: Reaction of an alcohol with organic acid is called the ____
questions.question_html: Reaction of an alcohol with organic acid is called the ____
questions.explanation: Esterification is the reaction in which a Carboxylic acid combines with an alcohol in the presence of little concentrated sulphuric acid to form an ester. The esters so formed are pleasant smelling.

questions.question_text: Alkenes react with water in the presence of acid as catalyst to form alcohols.
questions.question_html: Alkenes react with water in the presence of acid as catalyst to form alcohols.

options.text: nucleophilic attack of water on carbocation
options.html: nucleophilic attack of water on carbocation

questions.explanation: The addition of water to an alkene in the presence of a catalytic amount of strong acid leads to the formation of alcohols (hydroxy‐alkanes). {tex}C{H_2} = C{H_2} + {H_2}O\xrightarrow{{{H^ + }}}C{H_3}C{H_2}OH{/tex} This reaction proceeds via a standard carbocation mechanism and follows the Markovnikov rule. The mechanism for the addition of water to ethene follows. The hydrogen ion is attracted to the {tex}\pi{/tex} bond, which breaks to form a σ bond with one of the double-bonded carbons. The second carbon of the original double‐bonded carbons becomes a carbocation. An acid‐base reaction occurs between the water molecule and the carbocation, forming an oxonium ion. The oxonium ion stabilizes by losing a hydrogen ion, with the resulting formation of an alcohol.

questions.question_text: Ketones are reduced to the corresponding alcohols by catalytic hydrogenation to form _________.
questions.question_html: Ketones are reduced to the corresponding alcohols by catalytic hydrogenation to form _________.
questions.explanation: Carboxylic acids, esters, and acid halides can be reduced to either aldehydes or a step further to primary alcohols, depending on the strength of the reducing agent, aldehydes, and ketones can be reduced respectively to primary and secondary alcohols.

questions.question_text: What is the correct order of reactivity of alcohols in the following reaction? {tex}R - OH + HCl\xrightarrow{{ZnC{l_2}}}R - Cl + {H_2}O{/tex}
questions.question_html:
What is the correct order of reactivity of alcohols in the following reaction?

{tex}R - OH + HCl\xrightarrow{{ZnC{l_2}}}R - Cl + {H_2}O{/tex}
questions.explanation: The mixture of HCl and ZnCl 2 is called the Lucas Reagent. Secondary and tertiary alcohols react via the S N 1 mechanism with the Lucas reagent. The ZnCl 2 coordinates to the hydroxyl oxygen and this generates a far superior leaving group. When alcohols react with a hydrogen halide, a substitution occurs, producing an alkyl halide and water: Scope of Reaction: The order of reactivity of alcohols is 3° > 2° > 1°

questions.question_text: The process of converting alkyl halides into alcohols involves _____________.
questions.question_html: The process of converting alkyl halides into alcohols involves _____________.
questions.explanation: Alkyl halides are converted into alcohols by substitution reaction. When alkyl halides are treated with aqueous KOH, alcohols are formed. This is a nucleophilic substitution reaction.

questions.question_text: Which of the following compounds is aromatic alcohol?
questions.question_html: Which of the following compounds is aromatic alcohol?
questions.explanation: Compound (A) i.e., phenol and compound (D) i.e. a derivative of phenol cannot be considered as aromatic alcohol. As phenol is also known as, carbolic acid cannot be considered as aromatic alcohol. Benzyl alcohol and substituted benzyl alcohol are aromatic alcohol.

questions.question_text: Which of the following compounds will react with sodium hydroxide solution in water?
questions.question_html: Which of the following compounds will react with sodium hydroxide solution in water?
questions.explanation: Phenol is more soluble in NaOH than in water is because phenol is slightly more acidic than alcohols. The Ka for phenol in water is 1e-10 which is not very strong. But by mixing with NaOH, it causes the phenol to release the H+ to form sodium phenoxide.

questions.question_text: Which of the following species can act as the strongest base?
questions.question_html: Which of the following species can act as the strongest base?
questions.explanation: Hydroxy group is more electron donating group than alkoxy because alkoxy have greater number of atoms than that hydroxyl group there electronic density of oxygen of alkoxy group spreads over the whole group and greater the number of atom less will be the electron donating ability.

questions.question_text: Give IUPAC name of the compound given below. {tex}C{H_3} - \mathop {\mathop C\limits_| H}\limits_{Cl\;\;} - C{H_2} - C{H_2} - \mathop {\mathop C\limits_| H}\limits_{OH} - C{H_3}{/tex}
questions.question_html:
Give IUPAC name of the compound given below.

{tex}C{H_3} - \mathop {\mathop C\limits_| H}\limits_{Cl\;\;} - C{H_2} - C{H_2} - \mathop {\mathop C\limits_| H}\limits_{OH} - C{H_3}{/tex}
questions.explanation: {tex}\mathop {\mathop {C{H_3}}\limits^6 \mathop {\mathop {{\text{ }}C}\limits_|^5 H}\limits_{CL} - \mathop {C{H_2}}\limits^4 - \mathop {C{H_2}}\limits^3 - \mathop {\mathop {{\text{ }}C}\limits_|^2 H}\limits_{OH\;} - \mathop {{\text{ }}C}\limits^1 {H_2}}\limits_{5 - chlorohexan - 2 - ol} {/tex} -OH is given preference over -Cl so numbering is done so that –OH gets the lowest number.

questions.question_text: A hydrocarbon C 5 H 10 does not react with chlorine in dark but gives a single monochloro compound C 5 H 9 Cl in bright sunlight. The hydrocarbon is
questions.question_html: A hydrocarbon C5H10 does not react with chlorine in dark but gives a single monochloro compound C5H9Cl in bright sunlight. The hydrocarbon is
questions.explanation: Cyclopentane is nearly inert chemically, they react with halogens in the presence of light through the substitution of one hydrogen atoms. Since the cyclic structure confers a high degree of symmetry on the molecule, only one monochloro cyclopentane is possible.

questions.question_text: Phenol is less acidic than ______________.
questions.question_html: Phenol is less acidic than ______________.
questions.explanation: The nitro-group is an electron-withdrawing group. The presence of this group in the ortho or para position decreases the electron density in the OH bond. As a result, it is easier to lose a proton. Also, the o-nitrophenoxide or p-nitrophenoxide ion formed after the loss of protons is stabilized by resonance. Hence, ortho and para nitrophenols are stronger acids than phenol.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is most acidic?
questions.question_html: Which of the following is most acidic?
questions.explanation: In cases of halogen derivatives of phenols or aniline or benzoic acid etc, it is very helpful to understand that all halogens, when attached to benzene ring, exerts -I as well as +R effect. In case of Cl, Br and I, the +R effect has almost no effect on reactivity, acidic character or basic character of the benzene ring. It is due to very less effective overlapping involving 2p of carbon and 3p or 4p or 5p of halogen. Hence, only -I effect becomes the deciding factor, which is most dominant from ortho-position and least effective from para-position. So m chlorophenol is most acidic.

questions.question_text: Mark the correct order of decreasing acid strength of the following compounds.
questions.question_html: Mark the correct order of decreasing acid strength of the following compounds.
questions.explanation: The correct order of decreasing acid strength is b > d > a > c > e. As p-nitrophenol is most acidic and p-methoxy phenol is least acidic. When an electron-withdrawing group is para to the OH group, the acidity is maximum. When an electron releasing group is para to OH group, the acidity is minimum.

questions.question_text: Anisole can be prepared by the action of methyl iodide on sodium phenate. The reaction is called
questions.question_html: Anisole can be prepared by the action of methyl iodide on sodium phenate. The reaction is called
questions.explanation: The reaction of an alkyl halide with sodium alkoxide to give ether (alkoxy alkane), is known as Williamson's synthesis. In this reaction, an ether (anisole) is prepared by the action of alkyl halide (methyl iodide) on sodium alkoxide (sodium phenate), so it is an example of Williamson's synthesis.

questions.question_text: n – propyl bromide on treating with alcoholic KOH produces ______.
questions.question_html: n – propyl bromide on treating with alcoholic KOH produces ______.
questions.explanation: Alkenes can be prepared from alkyl halides by treatment with alcoholic solution of caustic potash (KOH) at about 353-363 K. This reaction is known as dehydrohalogenation of alkyl halides. {tex}\mathop {C{H_3}C{H_2}C{H_2}Br}\limits_{n - Propyl\;bromide} + KOH\xrightarrow[{353 - 363K}]{{{C_2}{H_5}OH}}\mathop {C{H_3}CH = C{H_2}}\limits_{Propene} + KBr + {H_2}O{/tex}

questions.question_text: Which of the following reactions will yield phenol?
questions.question_html: Which of the following reactions will yield phenol?
questions.explanation: Preparation of phenols from haloarenes : Chlorobenzene is an example of haloarenes which is formed by monosubstitution of the benzene ring. When chlorobenzene is fused with sodium hydroxide at 623K and 320 atm sodium phenoxide is produced. Finally, sodium phenoxide on acidification gives phenols. Preparation of phenols from diazonium salts: When an aromatic primary amine is treated with nitrous (NaNO 2 + HCl) acid at 273 - 278 K, diazonium salts are obtained. These diazonium salts are highly reactive in nature. Upon warming with water, these diazonium salts finally hydrolyse to phenols. Phenols can also be obtained from diazonium salts by treating it with dilute acids. Preparation of phenols from benzene sulphonic acid : Benzenesulphonic acid can be obtained from benzene by reacting it with oleum. Benzenesulphonic acid thus formed is treated with molten sodium hydroxide at high temperature which leads to the formation of sodium phenoxide. Finally, sodium phenoxide on acidification gives phenols.

questions.question_text: Which of the following reagents can be used to oxidise primary alcohols to aldehydes?
questions.question_html: Which of the following reagents can be used to oxidise primary alcohols to aldehydes?

options.text: Heat in the presence of Cu at 573K.
options.html: Heat in the presence of Cu at 573K.

questions.explanation: Oxidation of alcohols to aldehydes is partial oxidation ; aldehydes are further oxidized to carboxylic acids. Conditions required for making aldehydes are heat and distillation. In aldehyde formation, the temperature of the reaction should be kept above the boiling point of the aldehyde and below the boiling point of the alcohol. These include: Chromium-based reagents, such as Collins reagent (CrO 3 ·Py 2 ) PDC or PCC. Heat in the presence of Cu at 573K.

questions.question_text: Phenol can be distinguished from ethanol by the reactions with _________.
questions.question_html: Phenol can be distinguished from ethanol by the reactions with _________.
questions.explanation: Phenol and ethanol can be easily distinguished by the ferric chloride test. When to a solution of phenol, a few drops of ferric chloride is added, a purple colouration is observed due to the formation of the following complex. 3C 6 H 5 OH + FeCl 3 {tex}\to {/tex} Fe(OC 6 H 5 ) 3 + 3HCl Ethanol, on the other hand, gives no such test, (Alcohols do not form colored complexes with iron ion) as the corresponding complex in case of ethanol is not formed.

questions.question_text: How many alcohols with molecular formula C 4 H 10 O are chiral in nature?
questions.question_html: How many alcohols with molecular formula C4H10O are chiral in nature?
questions.explanation: A chiral molecule is one that is not superimposable on its mirror image. 2-Butanol is a small molecule with a chirality center. It is the simplest alcohol containing an asymmetric carbon. For a better illustration, the four different substituents are shown in different colours. Carbon atom C-2 of 2-butanol carries four different substituents H, CH 3 , OH, and CH 2 CH 3 . Exchange of any two of these substituents would yield the opposite enantiomer. However, such an exchange does not occur spontaneously because it requires a bond breakage. Therefore, enantiomers are different, stable, coexisting compounds.

questions.question_text: The preparation of ethers by acid dehydration of secondary or tertiary alcohols is not a suitable method. This is so because:
questions.question_html: The preparation of ethers by acid dehydration of secondary or tertiary alcohols is not a suitable method. This is so because:

options.text: Elimination competes over substitution and alkenes are easily formed.
options.html: Elimination competes over substitution and alkenes are easily formed.

questions.correct_option_text: Elimination competes over substitution and alkenes are easily formed.
questions.explanation: The formation of ethers by dehydration of the alcohol is a bimolecular reaction (S N 2 ) involving the attack of an alcohol molecule on a protonated alcohol molecule. In the method, the alkyl group should be unhindered. In the case of secondary or tertiary alcohols, the alkyl group is hindered. As a result, elimination dominates substitution as 3° carbocation is more stable. Hence, in place of ethers, alkenes are formed.

questions.question_text: Ethers can be prepared by Williamson synthesis in which an alkyl halide is reacted with sodium alkoxide. Di-tert-butyl ether can’t be prepared by this method. Explain.
questions.question_html: Ethers can be prepared by Williamson synthesis in which an alkyl halide is reacted with sodium alkoxide. Di-tert-butyl ether can’t be prepared by this method. Explain.

questions.question_text: Methyl bromide is converted into ethane by heating it in ether medium with:
questions.question_html: Methyl bromide is converted into ethane by heating it in ether medium with:
questions.explanation: The Wurtz reaction, named after Charles-Adolphe Wurtz, is a coupling reaction in organic chemistry, organometallic chemistry and recently inorganic main group polymers, whereby two alkyl halides are reacted with sodium metal in dry ether solution to form a higher alkane: 2R–X + 2Na {tex}\to {/tex} R–R + 2NaX.

questions.question_text: Which one of the following alcohol is poisonous and its ingestion of even small quantities can cause blindness and large quantities to cause even death?
questions.question_html: Which one of the following alcohol is poisonous and its ingestion of even small quantities can cause blindness and large quantities to cause even death?
questions.explanation: The most common cause of blindness from drinking is methanol. Methanol, otherwise known as methyl alcohol or wood alcohol, can damage the optic nerve and even kill you in high concentrations. During Prohibition, bootleggers were known to sell moonshine that contained methanol, and the practice continues abroad.

questions.question_text: The starting material for picric acid is,
questions.question_html: The starting material for picric acid is,
questions.explanation: To form pitric acid, Anhydrous phenol is sulfonated with fuming sulfuric acid, and the resulting p-hydroxyphenylsulfonic acid is then nitrated with concentrated nitric acid. During this reaction, nitro groups are introduced, and the sulfonic acid group is displaced.

questions.question_text: Glycerol is
questions.question_html: Glycerol is
questions.explanation: Glycerol and trihydroxypropane are common names. The IUPAC name is propane-1,2,3-triol. The common name glycerol comes from the root glyco- which means sweet. Glucose, glycogen, and glycerin (another name for glycerol) have the same etymology.

questions.question_text: Lucas reagent is:
questions.question_html: Lucas reagent is:
questions.explanation: Lucas reagent is a solution of anhydrous zinc chloride (Lewis acid) in concentrated hydrochloric acid. It is used as a reagent to test alcohol and classify them in accordance to their reactivity. The reaction is a substitution reaction where the chloride of the zinc chloride gets replaced by the hydroxyl group of the alcohol.
